objections.description: Anti-patterns table is incomplete and appears to be cut off. The row for 'Hamburger menus on desktop' ends mid-sentence with 'Full nav bar; reserve hamburger for' with no continuation.
objections.suggested_fix: Complete the anti-patterns table with all missing rows and finish the truncated row. Add more anti-patterns like: 'Placeholders instead of skeleton loaders', 'Modals for everything', 'Infinite scrolling without pagination options', 'Using color alone for state indication'.

objections.description: The skill mentions calling MCP tools (recommend_ui_sources, plan_ui_execution, generate_ui_agent_prompt) but provides no actual code examples showing how to invoke these tools. The parameter schemas are described as 'illustrative' but no actual function call patterns are shown.
objections.suggested_fix: Add concrete code examples showing how to call each MCP tool with proper parameter formatting. Example: `const sources = await mcp__ui-copilot__recommend_ui_sources({ task: userTask })` or similar. Show error handling and fallback logic in code.

objections.description: The table references galleries (curated.design, landing.love, etc.) but doesn't explain HOW these galleries inform recommendations. The skill claims to be 'powered by top UI galleries' but operates purely on built-in design knowledge.
objections.suggested_fix: Either remove the gallery references (since they're not actually used) or add a concrete mechanism showing how gallery patterns are fetched/integrated into recommendations.

objections.description: Tailwind v4 detection logic checks for `@theme` in CSS files, but this approach is incomplete. Many v4 projects may not have `@theme` blocks yet (using legacy config), and the detection doesn't account for hybrid setups.
objections.suggested_fix: Add more robust detection: check for `tailwind.config.js` existence, examine package.json for `@tailwindcss/vite` or `@tailwindcss/postcss`, and look for `@import 'tailwindcss'` directives in CSS.

objections.description: Missing several important component patterns despite the skill covering them: no modal/dialog examples (mentioned in z-index scale and navigation), no form validation examples, no accordion examples, no tabs component despite referencing 'tabbed showcase'.
objections.suggested_fix: Add code examples for: Modal/Dialog pattern, Form with floating labels + inline validation, Accordion pattern, Tabs component with implementation.

objections.description: States to 'silently fall back' on MCP tool failures but doesn't define what the fallback looks like in practice. The user experience may be confusing if they expect gallery-sourced recommendations.
objections.suggested_fix: Add a clear message or format indicating when built-in recommendations are being used versus MCP-sourced ones.

suggestions.description: The skill is very long (~6000 words). Consider splitting into a core skill + reference document structure, or creating sub-commands like /ui-savior hero, /ui-savior nav for common patterns.
Good accessibility coverage but could add: focus trap implementation example for modals, proper pattern for skip links beyond the single example shown, screen reader testing guidance.
The 8 design principles are solid but would benefit from being numbered and having brief 'why' explanations for each for quick reference.
Could add a 'When NOT to use this pattern' subsection to each decision tree, helping developers know when to deviate from recommendations.
Missing View Transitions API implementation details for the 'Page transition' row. This is now supported in Chrome and has good polyfill support.
